title: HTTPS and Secure Websites
drDigitalIntro: When a website is secure, its URL starts with https:// (the 's' stands for secure). A lock icon appears in the address bar. This means your connection is encrypted — information you send (passwords, credit card numbers, personal data) is scrambled and safe. If a site is just http:// without the 's', it's not secure. Never enter passwords or credit card numbers on an unsecured site.

playgroundTask.instructions: Which website is NOT safe to enter your password on?

items.label: https://amazon.com
items.preview: 🔒 amazon.com — Secure checkout. Trusted retailer since 1995.

items.label: http://amaz0n-deals.net
items.preview: ⚠️ No lock icon. amaz0n-deals.net — HUGE SALE click to buy now!!!

items.label: https://apple.com
items.preview: 🔒 apple.com — Official Apple Store. Shop with confidence.

playgroundTask.fakeExplanation: http:// (no 's') means no encryption, and the misspelled URL ('amaz0n' with a zero) is a classic phishing trick.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted it! Always look for https:// and the 🔒 lock icon before entering sensitive information.
drDigitalHint: Look for two things: does it say https (with an s)? Is the domain name spelled correctly?
